content.problemStatement:
Define a **block** of a binary string to be a maximal substring consisting of the same character. For example, the blocks of 1100100\text{1100100}1100100 are 11\text{11}11, 00\text{00}00, 1\text{1}1 and 00\text{00}00.

You are given a string SSS, of size NNN, consisting of characters 000, 111 or ???. It is guaranteed that SSS does not begin or end with a ???, i.e. S1‚Č†?S_1 \ne ?S1‚ÄčÓÄ†=? and SN‚Č†?S_N \ne ?SN‚ÄčÓÄ†=?.

Your job is to replace each ??? with either 000 or 111, and then obtain a binary string with exactly KKK ones. Among all valid strings with exactly KKK ones, find the **minimum number of blocks possible**.

You must solve the above problem for each K=0,1,2,‚Ä¶,NK = 0, 1, 2, \ldots, NK=0,1,2,‚Ä¶,N. For certain KKK, it may be impossible and you should print ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 instead.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains a single integer NNN - the size of the string SSS.
The second line contains a string SSS.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line N+1N + 1N+1 integers, the answers for K=0,1,2,‚Ä¶,NK = 0, 1, 2, \ldots, NK=0,1,2,‚Ä¶,N.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : It is impossible to have K=2K = 2K=2 or K=3K = 3K=3 because there is only 111 ??? to replace and no already filled 111.
For K=0K = 0K=0, the only string is 000000000 which has 111 block.
For K=1K = 1K=1, the only string is 010010010 which has 333 blocks.
